title: ActivityWatch vs Timely for Solo users
meta_description: See why Timely fails first when tracking time requires syncing data to cloud services and maintaining an online account and why ActivityWatch is the better pick for solo users.
constraint_lens: This person wants time tracking that runs locally without relying on accounts or cloud syncing.

one_second_verdict.summary: Best for Solo users who want fully local time tracking without cloud accounts.
one_second_verdict.reason: Timely fails first because it requires syncing data to cloud services and maintaining an online account.

verdict.summary: ActivityWatch is the better choice when you want private time tracking that runs entirely on your device. It stores all activity data locally and does not require an account or cloud syncing. Timely relies on cloud services and account-based access, which adds dependency and ongoing connection requirements.
verdict.decision_rule: If tracking time requires syncing data to cloud services and maintaining an online account, Timely fails first.

sections.heading: Why ActivityWatch fits Solo users better
sections.content: ActivityWatch fits this solo user because the same local-first mechanism removes friction in several places at once. It lowers setup by removing account creation, keeps daily tracking available even without a connection, and reduces the background worry that sync or login state will interrupt normal use. The win is not only privacy; it is steadier day-to-day operation with less upkeep.

bullets.point: ActivityWatch starts without asking you to maintain an online account
bullets.why_it_matters: That removes an entire layer of setup and ongoing credential management before tracking can even feel routine.

bullets.point: ActivityWatch keeps day-to-day tracking usable even when you are offline
bullets.why_it_matters: The timer or activity log still works when your connection drops instead of turning basic capture into a sync problem.

bullets.point: ActivityWatch keeps the tool mentally lighter over time
bullets.why_it_matters: There is less to monitor because local capture does not keep surfacing account status, sync state, or browser dependency.

bullets.point: Timely is easier when you need the same data on multiple devices
bullets.why_it_matters: Cloud access can be genuinely helpful if your tracking does not live on one machine.

bullets.point: Timely is simpler for sharing or checking time from a browser
bullets.why_it_matters: Hosted access helps when the value comes from availability rather than from local control.

bullets.point: Timely shifts storage and sync mechanics out of your hands
bullets.why_it_matters: That can feel lighter if you prefer convenience over owning the environment yourself.

items.fails_when: ActivityWatch becomes limiting when the same time data has to stay visible across several devices or be checked from anywhere with no local machine involved.
items.what_to_do_instead: Choose Timely if cross-device access matters more than local-only simplicity.

items.fails_when: Timely breaks down when account maintenance, sync state, or online dependency keeps getting in the way of a tool that should feel invisible.
items.what_to_do_instead: Choose ActivityWatch when local capture and lower upkeep matter more than hosted availability.

sections.heading: When this verdict might flip
sections.content: This can flip if the user regularly moves between devices or needs browser access from anywhere more than they need local-only simplicity. In that case, Timely can justify the extra account layer.

sections.heading: Quick rules
rules: Choose ActivityWatch if you want local capture without account upkeep.
Choose Timely if browser access or multi-device visibility matters more.
Avoid Timely when sync and login state are becoming the real maintenance burden.

faqs.q: Why does local first help beyond privacy?
faqs.a: It also reduces account upkeep, lowers dependence on sync, and keeps basic tracking available when connectivity is not the thing you want to think about.

faqs.q: What do you give up with ActivityWatch?
faqs.a: Usually some convenience around browser access, easier device switching, or hosted sharing.

faqs.q: When is Timely still worth choosing?
faqs.a: Timely is still worth choosing when availability across devices matters more than keeping the tracker self-contained.

faqs.q: What is the warning sign that Timely is the wrong fit?
faqs.a: It is when the account and sync layer keeps creating more maintenance than the actual time tracking itself.
